sources.title: VS Code — Customize AI responses (overview)
sources.whyItMatters: Canonical entry point defining all six customization primitives (instructions, agents, skills, prompts, hooks, subagents), their file locations, and the Chat Customizations editor.

sources.title: VS Code — Custom instructions
sources.whyItMatters: Defines two instruction types (always-on copilot-instructions.md vs file-based .instructions.md with applyTo globs), personal > repository > organization priority, and tips for effective writing (short, self-contained, include reasoning, show patterns, focus on non-obvious rules).

sources.title: VS Code — Custom agents
sources.whyItMatters: Defines .agent.md format with YAML frontmatter fields (description, name, tools, agents, model, user-invocable, disable-model-invocation, target, mcp-servers, handoffs, hooks), agent file locations, handoff workflows, and security considerations.

sources.title: VS Code — Agent skills
sources.whyItMatters: Defines SKILL.md format, progressive loading (discovery→instructions→resources), skill file locations, frontmatter fields (name, description, argument-hint, user-invocable, disable-model-invocation), the Agent Skills open standard (agentskills.io), and vs-instructions comparison table.

sources.title: VS Code — Prompt files
sources.whyItMatters: Defines .prompt.md format, frontmatter fields (description, name, argument-hint, agent, model, tools), tool list priority (prompt > agent > default), input variables, and that prompt files are being superseded by skills for complex workflows.

sources.title: VS Code — Agent hooks
sources.whyItMatters: Defines hook lifecycle events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, Stop), hook file locations (.github/hooks/*.json), agent-scoped hooks in frontmatter, input/output JSON format, exit codes, and security considerations.

sources.title: VS Code — Copilot extensibility overview
sources.whyItMatters: Positions the entire customization framework: instructions → agents → skills → hooks → MCP → extensions, showing how each layer adds capability and complexity.

sources.title: GitHub — Customizing Copilot (custom instructions)
sources.whyItMatters: GitHub-side documentation for repository custom instructions (.github/copilot-instructions.md), organization-level instructions, and instruction priority rules on github.com surfaces.

sources.title: GitHub — Copilot coding agent (Copilot Workspace)
sources.whyItMatters: Covers the remote coding agent that runs in GitHub Actions, including how it reads custom instructions, respects tool restrictions, and handles file-scoped instructions.

sources.title: VS Code — Subagents documentation
sources.whyItMatters: Explains subagent delegation, context isolation, the agents field in .agent.md, handoff patterns, and how subagents interact with hooks (SubagentStart/SubagentStop events).

sources.title: VS Code — Agent tools documentation
sources.whyItMatters: Defines tool categories, tool list priority (prompt > agent > default), tool approval flow, and how to restrict or expand tool access per agent.

sources.title: VS Code — Release notes (Copilot customization updates)
sources.whyItMatters: Tracks capability shifts: skills availability (1.100+), hooks preview, agent-scoped hooks, prompt files superseded by skills, settings-based instructions deprecated (1.102), model field additions.

sources.title: Anthropic — Introducing advanced tool use on the Claude Developer Platform
sources.whyItMatters: Defines three tool design patterns that apply directly to Copilot customization: Tool Search Tool (on-demand discovery via defer_loading, analogous to progressive skill loading), Programmatic Tool Calling (context-efficient orchestration via code, analogous to subagent delegation), and Tool Use Examples (input_examples for parameter accuracy, analogous to concrete invocation patterns in skill descriptions). Internal testing: tool examples improved accuracy from 72% to 90%, programmatic calling reduced tokens by 37%.

sources.title: Anthropic — Programmatic tool calling documentation
sources.whyItMatters: Detailed API documentation for programmatic tool calling: allowed_callers, tool description best practices (document output format, return structured data, keep responses concise), batch processing patterns, early termination, conditional tool selection. The best-practices section directly informs how to write agent/skill descriptions: provide detailed output descriptions, return structured data, keep responses concise.
